ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.
ait: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikem.
ane: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xârâcùù.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
ant: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Antakarinya.
ante: noun. A price or cost, as in up the ante.
ate: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Atemble.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
nae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Naka'ela.
neat: adjective. Clean, tidy; free from dirt or impurities.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nett: adjective. Alternative spelling of net (remaining after expenses or deductions).
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
tae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tariana.
taint: noun. A contamination, decay or putrefaction, especially in food.
tat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tatar.
tate: noun. Of video games, a vertical mode.
tea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Temiar.
teat: noun. The projection of a mammary gland from which, on female therian mammals, milk is secreted.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tet: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tetum.
tit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tinigua.
titan: noun. Something or someone of very large stature, greatness, or godliness.
titi: noun. Any New World monkey of the subfamily Callicebinae, native to South America, distinguished by their long soft fur.
titian: noun. A bright auburn colour, tinted with gold.
